name: Front Porch
description: This shallow reef slopes gently to 18 meters, teeming with colorful sponges, soft corals, and schools of tropical fish. Ideal for beginners, the calm conditions and excellent visibility make it perfect for spotting seahorses, octopus, and juvenile marine life. The sandy patches between coral formations host garden eels and flamingo tongues. Easy shore access adds to its appeal.

name: South Bay
description: This shallow, sheltered bay offers easy shore diving with vibrant coral gardens and abundant marine life. The sandy bottom slopes gently to 15 meters, perfect for beginners. Expect parrotfish, angelfish, and seahorses among the soft corals. Strong sunlight enhances visibility, often exceeding 30 meters. Calm conditions make it ideal for night dives to spot octopus and lobster. The nearby mangroves attract juvenile fish, adding diversity. Minimal current allows relaxed exploration.

name: Hands Off
description: This dive site off Bonaire features a stunning coral-covered slope starting at 12 meters, dropping to 30 meters. Vibrant sponges and sea fans thrive here, attracting schools of tropical fish, parrotfish, and occasional turtles. The current is mild, making it ideal for drift diving. The name comes from a large anchor embedded in the reef—look but don’t touch. Excellent visibility and warm water year-round create perfect conditions for relaxed exploration.

name: Carl's Hill (Punt P'abou)
description: This site features a stunning coral-covered slope starting at 10 meters, dropping to 30 meters. Expect vibrant sponges, sea fans, and schools of tropical fish like parrotfish and angelfish. The current is mild, making it ideal for drift diving. Look for moray eels hiding in crevices and occasional turtles gliding by. The clear water and abundant marine life create excellent visibility year-round. Easy shore access adds to its appeal.

name: Bachelor's Beach
description: This shallow, sandy entry site is perfect for beginners and shore diving. The reef starts close to shore, sloping gently to 18 meters. Expect vibrant coral formations, schools of tropical fish, and frequent turtle sightings. The calm conditions and easy access make it ideal for training dives or relaxed exploration. Night dives here reveal octopus and lobster activity.

name: La Dania's Leap
description: This site is a dramatic wall dive starting at a rocky point. Descend along steep cliffs covered in vibrant corals. Expect frequent sightings of turtles, tarpon, and reef sharks. The current can be strong, making it ideal for drift diving. Excellent visibility reveals massive sponges and intricate overhangs teeming with tropical fish. Depth ranges from 5 to 40 meters, suitable for all skill levels. The entry is a thrilling leap from the shore. Night dives showcase bioluminescent creatures.

name: Cai
description: This site off Bonaire features a vibrant coral reef teeming with parrotfish, angelfish, and occasional turtles. The gentle slope makes it ideal for beginners, with clear visibility and minimal current. Sponges and soft corals add bursts of color, while small caves attract curious critters. Depths range from 5 to 20 meters, perfect for relaxed exploration. The sandy bottom near the shore transitions to healthy reef formations further out. Warm Caribbean waters and easy shore access enhance the experience. Night dives reveal octopuses and lobsters.

name: Vista Blue
description: This shallow reef slopes gently to 18 meters, teeming with vibrant coral formations. Schools of parrotfish and angelfish dart among the sponges, while seahorses cling to seagrass beds. The calm, clear waters make it ideal for beginners and photographers. Night dives reveal octopuses and lobsters emerging from crevices. Strong currents are rare, allowing relaxed exploration. The site sits just offshore, accessible from the beach.

name: Sharon's Serenity
description: A gentle slope of hard coral leads to a vibrant reef teeming with parrotfish, angelfish, and occasional turtles. Clear Caribbean waters offer excellent visibility year-round. The shallow entry makes it ideal for beginners, while deeper sections reveal small caves and overhangs. Night dives showcase octopus and lobster activity. Easy shore access with minimal current.

name: Munk's Heaven
description: This site off Bonaire's coast features a stunning wall dive starting at 12 meters, dropping to over 30 meters. Vibrant coral formations cover the slope, with frequent sightings of eagle rays, turtles, and reef sharks. The current is mild, making it ideal for drift diving. Sponges and sea fans add bursts of color, while schools of tropical fish swirl around the reef. Excellent visibility and warm water year-round create perfect conditions for all skill levels.

name: Jeannie's Glory
description: This vibrant reef dive in Bonaire features a stunning coral garden teeming with life. Schools of tropical fish dart between healthy elkhorn and brain corals. Gentle slopes lead to sandy patches where garden eels peek out. The site offers excellent visibility and calm conditions, perfect for beginners. Keep an eye out for turtles gliding past the occasional nurse shark resting below. Night dives reveal octopus and lobster activity. Easy shore access makes it a favorite for repeat visits.

name: Bloodlet
description: This site offers shallow coral reefs teeming with colorful marine life, perfect for relaxed diving. The underwater terrain features sandy patches, sponges, and small caves. Often calm conditions make it ideal for beginners or photographers. Look for seahorses, parrotfish, and eels among the healthy coral formations. The clear Caribbean waters provide excellent visibility year-round. No strong currents here, allowing leisurely exploration.
